channel.title: Layer on ErrorCode — Error Code Reference & Solutions
channel.description: Recent content in Layer on ErrorCode — Error Code Reference & Solutions

item.title: [Solution] Docker Build Cache Error — build cache error
item.description:
A Docker build cache error occurs when the build cache is corrupted or incompatible. This can cause builds to fail or produce unexpected results.

Common Causes

  • Corrupted build cache from an interrupted build
  • Cache mount points not available during build
  • BuildKit cache backend issues
  • Insufficient disk space for cache storage
  • Cross-platform build cache incompatibility

How to Fix

Prune Build Cache

docker builder prune

Clear All Build Cache

docker builder prune -a

Disable Build Cache

docker build --no-cache -t my-image .

Check BuildKit Status

DOCKER_BUILDKIT=1 docker build -t my-image .

Set Cache Export/Import

docker build --cache-from type=registry,ref=my-image:latest -t my-image .

Examples

# Example 1: Clear and rebuild
docker builder prune -a
docker build -t my-image .

# Example 2: Build without cache
docker build --no-cache -t my-image .

# Example 3: Use BuildKit for better caching
DOCKER_BUILDKIT=1 docker build --progress=plain -t my-image .

Related Errors

  • Docker Image Not Found — image not found in registry
  • Docker Pull Timeout — Docker pull timeout

item.title: [Solution] Docker Build Cache Error — Fix BuildKit Cache Failures
item.description:
Docker Build Cache Error — Fix BuildKit Cache Failures

A Docker build cache error occurs when docker build fails due to a corrupted or inaccessible build cache. The error reads:

“error: failed to solve: error getting build cache: reference not found”

Or:

“WARNING: buildx: cache export error: No such image”

What This Error Means

Docker builds images in layers. Each Dockerfile instruction creates a new layer, and Docker caches these layers to speed up subsequent builds. When the cache becomes corrupted, references are lost, or the cache export/import fails, the build fails. BuildKit (the default builder in Docker 23+) uses a more sophisticated cache backend that can encounter these issues.

item.title: [Solution] Linux docker build Failed — No Space Left on Device
item.description:
Linux: docker build — no space left on device

The no space left on device error during docker build means the host filesystem has run out of disk space. Docker images consist of layers stored on the local filesystem, and the build process creates intermediate layers that consume space even after the final image is created.

What This Error Means

Docker stores images, containers, and build cache in /var/lib/docker by default. During a build, each RUN command creates a new layer. Temporary files, package manager caches, and build artifacts within layers all consume disk space. When the filesystem runs dry, the build fails with ENOSPC (no space left on device).
